First, food was often scarce. Hunters and gatherers might have no problem
finding food during a warm spring month, but then spend several months
starving during a harsh winter. To protect us against the uncertainty of when
our next meal was coming, our bodies would store as much fat as possible
during the good times. That way we could burn through it during the hard
times. The second reason our fat cells’ “default mode” is to hold fat is because
for 95% of human history we weren’t at the top of the food chain. Insulting, I
know, but the truth is we used to be routinely hunted by predators like lions,
leopards, and even crocodiles, and when you’re running through the savanna
on 105 degree day in July, with a majestic-but-terrifyingly-hungry lion hot on
your tail, you better have a lot of energy ready to burn. Otherwise, you’re going
to end up as some big cat’s human-flavored Meow Mix.
(16:14)
Thanks to our stores of fat, we did have enough energy, and the result is that while
some of our unlucky ancestors got eaten by predators, most of them were able

TRPV1’s main job is to regulate your body’s temperature and energy usage.

like intense cardio, this activity increases your blood flow, you start sweating,
and your body temperature goes up. Because TRPV1 is like an energy efficient

your fat cells to start bringing their content for energy. That way, your body
can handle the physical stress you’re putting on it without you overheating

found in human and animal studies to help silence ghrelin (the hunger hormone),

the bloodstream) levels in check support healthy cholesterol levels, support a
healthy liver, and promote circulation and a healthy heart. In other words, your
TRPV1 receptors love Capsaicin so much so, in fact, that scientists frequently
call TRPV1 receptors by another name, and that name is “Capsaicin receptors,”
which makes perfect sense since they are so closely-tied together.
(26:20)
On the one hand, it’s pretty crazy, because why do we humans have some built

activated from the capsaicin that’s inside chili peppers, and when this activation
